title: Expose all src edit tools and remove replace-only bias in proposal execution
summary: Surface the full src edit toolset in tool definitions and align execution guidance/filtering/stall tracking so approved src proposals can reliably use insert/delete/write src operations, not just replace-based edits.
details:
## Why this change
- Current proposal execution guidance still biases src edits toward replace-only operations (`find_replace_source (preferred) or replace_lines_source`), which steers execution even when insert/delete/create semantics are safer.
- Source evidence:
  - `src/gateway/routes/proposals.router.ts` line ~163 hardcodes replace-first guidance.
  - `src/gateway/tools/defs/file-web-memory.ts` only exposes `find_replace_source` and `replace_lines_source` in the src write section.
  - `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts` already supports additional src tools (`insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, `write_source`).
  - `src/gateway/teams/team-dispatch-runtime.ts` non-proposer blocklist currently only includes two src write tools.
  - `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts` mutate-tool stall reset list only includes two src write tools.

## Exact source edits
- `src/gateway/tools/defs/file-web-memory.ts` (src editing tool definitions section, around lines 218-256):
  - Current flow summary: defines only `find_replace_source` and `replace_lines_source` for src writes.
  - Planned edit: add full JSON-schema tool definitions for:
    - `insert_after_source(file, after_line, content)`
    - `delete_lines_source(file, start_line, end_line)`
    - `write_source(file, content, overwrite?)`
  - Keep existing descriptions, proposal-session constraints, and path semantics consistent with executor behavior.

- `src/gateway/routes/proposals.router.ts` (dispatch prompt assembly, around lines 130-165):
  - Current flow summary: src tool block lists insert/delete but instructions line still says `find_replace_source (preferred) or replace_lines_source`.
  - Planned edit: change instruction #2 to operation-based guidance, e.g. use the src tool matching the edit type (find/replace, line replace, insert, delete, create) rather than replace-first preference.

- `src/gateway/teams/team-dispatch-runtime.ts` (tool blocklist for non-proposers, around lines 139-144):
  - Current flow summary: when `!canPropose`, blocks `write_proposal`, `find_replace_source`, `replace_lines_source` only.
  - Planned edit: also block `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, and `write_source` to keep gating parity.

- `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts` (MUTATE_TOOLS constant, around lines 48-54):
  - Current flow summary: stall-reset mutation set includes only `find_replace_source` + `replace_lines_source` for src edits.
  - Planned edit: include `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, and `write_source` so legitimate src write progress resets stall detection.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
- Happy path:
  - Approved src proposals can select the most appropriate src write primitive (replace/line-replace/insert/delete/write) without prompt bias toward replace-only edits.
  - Tool schema advertised to the model matches what executor already supports.
- Edge cases:
  - Non-proposer team agents remain blocked from all src write tools uniformly.
  - Stall heuristics no longer misclassify insert/delete/write src operations as non-progress.
- Error handling:
  - Existing executor session/path guards remain unchanged (proposal/code_exec session requirement and src path enforcement).
- Explicit non-changes:
  - No change to src tool authorization model.
  - No change to proposal approval flow or task creation semantics.

## Acceptance tests
1. Build/typecheck:
   - Run `npm run build` and confirm successful compile.
2. Tool exposure verification:
   - Confirm tool list includes `insert_after_source`, `delete_lines_source`, `write_source` alongside existing src tools in proposal execution context.
3. Prompt behavior verification:
   - Approve a dummy src proposal and verify generated execution instructions do not say `find_replace_source (preferred)` and instead direct operation-based tool choice.
4. Gating + runtime checks:
   - Verify non-proposer team dispatch blocks all five src write tools.
   - Verify background runner `MUTATE_TOOLS` includes all five src write tools.

## Risks and compatibility
- Potential risks:
  - Slight behavior shift in executor model tool selection due to broader surfaced options.
- Backward compatibility:
  - Fully backward compatible; existing replace-based flows continue to work.
- Mitigations/rollback:
  - Keep existing replace tools intact; rollback is straightforward by reverting the four touched files.

affectedFiles.description: Expose insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, and write_source tool definitions for src edit sessions.
Replace replace-preferred instruction with operation-based src tool guidance.
Expand non-proposer blocklist to include all src write tools for parity.
Treat all src write tools as mutate events for stall-reset tracking.

diffPreview:
- Instruction: "find_replace_source (preferred) or replace_lines_source"
+ Instruction: "Use the src tool that matches the edit operation: find_replace_source, replace_lines_source, insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, or write_source (for new src files)."

- Tool defs: [find_replace_source, replace_lines_source]
+ Tool defs: [find_replace_source, replace_lines_source, insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, write_source]

- Team non-proposer blocklist: [write_proposal, find_replace_source, replace_lines_source]
+ Team non-proposer blocklist: [write_proposal, find_replace_source, replace_lines_source, insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, write_source]

- MUTATE_TOOLS src subset: [find_replace_source, replace_lines_source]
+ MUTATE_TOOLS src subset: [find_replace_source, replace_lines_source, insert_after_source, delete_lines_source, write_source]
estimatedImpact: Removes src-edit execution bias, aligns surfaced tools with executor capabilities, and improves reliability of proposal execution/stall handling.
executorPrompt:
Execute this approved src-edit proposal deterministically:
1) Edit only the listed affected files and only for the described purpose.
2) In src/ paths, use src edit tools appropriate to operation type.
3) Keep authorization/session guard behavior unchanged.
4) Run npm run build once after edits; if build fails, stop and report exact error.
5) Return an exact changed-files summary and what was verified.
